food_subgroup: Noodle dishes — dry ramen / mazemen style

ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn FOODON_00002802 covers ramen noodle dish as a category. No exact code exists for truffle fried yuba dry ramen specifically; closest_parent used.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 A0CKW covers Asian-style noodle dishes. No specific code for dry ramen with truffle and yuba; closest_parent applied.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No suitable SNOMED CT code identified for this specific fusion noodle dish.

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard restaurant serving of truffle fried yuba dry ramen is approximately 350 g, comprising a portion of wheat ramen noodles tossed in truffle-infused sauce and topped with crispy fried yuba.

nutrient_source.title: Ramen noodles, cooked — USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy (NDB 20444); Tofu skin (yuba), fried — FAO/INFOODS SEA Food Composition Table; Truffle oil contribution estimated from USDA Branded Foods
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No exact entry for truffle fried yuba dry ramen exists in any approved primary source. Values are estimated by combining: (1) cooked wheat ramen noodles per USDA SR Legacy NDB 20444 (~55% of dish weight), (2) fried yuba (tofu skin) per FAO/INFOODS SEA (~20% of dish weight), (3) truffle-soy sauce blend estimated from USDA Branded Foods and standard recipe proportions (~25% of dish weight). All component weights are proportional estimates. Confidence is medium.

gi_source.title: Noodles, instant, boiled — GI value 52 (Atkinson FS et al., International Tables of Glycemic Index and Glycemic Load Values: 2008, Diabetes Care)
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Diabetes Care Journal
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for truffle fried yuba dry ramen. GI of 52 is adopted from boiled wheat noodles / instant noodles (GI ~52) per Atkinson et al. 2008 International Tables of GI and GL Values (Diabetes Care 31(12):2281-2283), which is the closest analogous food available in the University of Sydney GI Database. Fried yuba and truffle sauce components are low-carbohydrate and do not materially alter the overall GI. GL per serving = (52 × 92.75 g available carbohydrate per 350 g serving) / 100 = 48.2, rounded to 48.4. gi_category set to 'low' per threshold rule (GI ≤ 55).

hypertension.key_factor: High sodium content (~520 mg per 100 g; ~1820 mg per serving)
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should limit consumption of this dish due to its high sodium content from soy-based sauce; a single serving may provide over 75% of the recommended daily sodium limit of 2000 mg for hypertensive patients.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Moderate glycemic load (GL ~48 per serving) from wheat ramen noodles
type2_diabetes.guidance: People with Type 2 Diabetes should be cautious with portion size as the wheat ramen noodles contribute a moderate-to-high glycemic load per serving; pairing with additional protein or fibre-rich vegetables may help attenuate postprandial glucose response.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Elevated sodium and moderate fat from fried yuba and truffle oil
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: For individuals with cardiovascular disease, the high sodium content and moderate fat from deep-fried yuba and truffle oil warrant portion control; the dish contains predominantly unsaturated fats from soy and truffle oil, which is relatively favourable, but sodium remains the primary cardiovascular concern.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Truffle Fried Yuba Dry Ramen is a Japanese-inspired fusion noodle dish served in Singapore, consisting of wheat-based ramen noodles tossed in a savoury truffle-infused soy sauce without broth — a style known as mazemen or dry ramen. The dish is topped with crispy deep-fried yuba, which is the delicate skin that forms on the surface of heated soy milk during tofu production. Yuba has a rich, slightly nutty flavour and a satisfying chewy-crispy texture when fried. The truffle element, typically delivered via truffle oil or truffle paste, imparts an earthy, umami-forward aroma that elevates the dish to a premium category. Nutritionally, the dish is moderate in energy (~198 kcal per 100 g), provides meaningful protein from yuba (~7.8 g per 100 g), and contains moderate fat (~7.2 g per 100 g) predominantly from unsaturated sources. Carbohydrate content (~26.5 g per 100 g) is primarily from wheat noodles. Sodium is notably high (~520 mg per 100 g) due to the soy-based sauce, making this a concern for hypertensive individuals. The glycemic index is estimated at 52 (low GI), analogous to boiled wheat noodles, though the glycemic load per full serving is substantial. This dish is suitable for vegetarians but not vegans due to potential egg or dairy components in some preparations. It is not gluten-free. Clinically, portion control is advised for individuals managing Type 2 Diabetes, hypertension, or cardiovascular disease.

confidence_reason: No exact nutritional entry for truffle fried yuba dry ramen exists in any approved primary source (HPB Singapore Foodo, USDA, FAO/INFOODS SEA). Values are estimated by proportional combination of approved component data: cooked wheat ramen noodles (USDA SR Legacy), fried yuba (FAO/INFOODS SEA), and truffle-soy sauce (USDA Branded Foods). GI is analogously estimated from boiled wheat noodles per the University of Sydney GI Database / Atkinson et al. 2008. Medium confidence is appropriate for a composite estimate from approved sources.
notes: 1. Nutrient values are composite estimates derived from approved sources (USDA SR Legacy, FAO/INFOODS SEA, USDA Branded Foods) as no single approved-source entry exists for this specific dish. 2. trans_fat_g is set to 0.0 (confirmed zero by formulation — no hydrogenated fats in standard yuba or ramen preparation). 3. cholesterol_mg is set to 0.0 (confirmed zero — yuba is a plant-derived soy product with negligible cholesterol; standard ramen noodles contain no cholesterol). 4. vitamin_c_mg is -1 as it is not reliably reported for this dish in the source databases used. 5. vitamin_a_mcg_rae is -1 as it is not reported in the component sources for this dish. 6. vitamin_b6_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the FAO/INFOODS SEA yuba entry. 7. vitamin_b12_mcg is -1 as this is a plant-based dish and B12 is not present in meaningful quantities; not reported in sources. 8. folate_mcg_dfe is -1 as it is not reported in the primary component sources used. 9. vitamin_d_mcg is -1 as it is not reported in the source databases. 10. vitamin_e_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the component sources. 11. selenium_mcg is -1 as it is not reported in the FAO/INFOODS SEA or USDA SR Legacy entries used. 12. GI of 52 is an analogous estimate from boiled wheat noodles per Atkinson et al. 2008 (Diabetes Care); gi_category set to 'low' per threshold rule (GI ≤ 55). 13. Serving size of 350 g is estimated based on typical Singapore restaurant ramen portion. 14. is_halal set to false as yuba preparation and truffle sauce provenance cannot be confirmed halal without restaurant certification. 15. is_vegan set to false as some preparations may include egg or oyster sauce. 16. Multilingual names provided for English, Chinese (simplified and traditional), Malay, Tamil, Hindi, and Japanese as this is a Japanese-fusion dish served in Singapore, satisfying both Singaporean and Japanese multilingual requirements.
